strands.title: Local execution context and caveats
strands.content: User operates in local command execution context. Prior local command output should not be treated as conversational input requiring response. The local-command-caveat is explicitly and repeatedly invoked, indicating user is aware of and enforcing the distinction between local state changes and conversational turns. This pattern suggests deliberate state management rather than accidental command output.
key_claims: Local-command-caveat has been re-invoked in cycle 3
User is explicitly managing the boundary between local operations and conversational content

strands.title: Working directory and project setup
strands.content: User (tony) has established ~/projects (/home/tony/projects) as a working directory with permissions management available. This state remains current and unchanged in cycle 3.
key_claims: ~/projects directory is established as active working context

strands.title: Model configuration state
strands.content: Local model configuration has been REAFFIRMED to claude-opus-4-6 in cycle 3. The /model command was re-executed with identical arguments, suggesting either confirmation of prior setting, verification of state, or preparation for imminent work. Model remains Opus throughout cycles 2-3.
key_claims: Model is set to claude-opus-4-6 (reaffirmed in cycle 3)
Command was executed a second time with same arguments

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Semantic distinction between initial model set (cycle 2) and model reaffirmation (cycle 3)
declared_losses.why: Both commands are identical (/model claude-opus-4-6); the integrated state cannot distinguish intent difference without explicit user explanation
Local command caveat applies; only state transition is retained, not execution timeline details

open_questions: Why was the /model command re-executed with identical arguments in cycle 3? Does this indicate: (a) verification behavior, (b) deliberate re-confirmation before action, (c) system state recovery, or (d) user testing the command?
Will the next conversational or command turn represent the beginning of actual project work within ~/projects?
Is there significance to the explicit local-command-caveat re-invocation that signals a change in user intent or meta-awareness?
What substantive work or query will the Opus model configuration enable in upcoming cycles?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "Continue treating all local-command-caveat flagged messages as non-conversational state changes",
  "Remain sensitive to pivot signals: if user begins actual conversation (non-command), activate full attention to substantive queries",
  "If next turn involves project work, queries, or conversational content, leverage integrated state (model=Opus, working_dir=~/projects) without re-explanation",
  "Monitor for any deviation from Opus configuration or ~/projects working directory that might indicate state change",
  "Be prepared for immediate substantive engagement if conversation begins; configuration setup phase appears to be concluding"
]
